title: circaea alpina subsp. intermedia
intro: Circaea alpina subsp. intermedia, commonly known as the marsh bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Diapensiaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows in wetlands and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of marsh bellflower include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Marsh bellflower is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
